**One-Year Practice Schedule**

*   **Months 1-6 (Foundation):** Focus on technique and basics. Read sheet music daily, practice scales, and learn chords. Aim to play simple melodies confidently by the end of year one.
*   **Months 7-9 (Intermediate):** Explore repertoire pieces of moderate difficulty. Attend masterclasses or join ensemble groups. Record yourself monthly to review progress.
*   **Months 10-12 (Performance):** Prepare a setlist for performance. Refine stage presence and technical perfection. Seek feedback from mentors regularly. Maintain consistency throughout.

This plan prioritizes skill growth over arbitrary letter constraints.<|im_end|>

final_answer:
It is not possible to fulfill both constraints simultaneously because asking for a letter to appear at least 20 times and at most 18 times creates a logical contradiction. No amount of text can satisfy both conditions. 

However, I can outline a general practice schedule for mastery. Please note that due to this impossibility, I am not adjusting the text to match a specific M-count, as doing so would violate the rule prohibiting a minimum of 20 when a maximum of 18 is also required.

**One-Year Practice Schedule**

*   **Months 1-6 (Foundation):** Focus on technique and basics. Read sheet music daily, practice scales, and learn chords. Aim to play simple melodies confidently by the end of year one.
*   **Months 7-9 (Intermediate):** Explore repertoire pieces of moderate difficulty. Attend masterclasses or join ensemble groups. Record yourself monthly to review progress.
*   **Months 10-12 (Performance):** Prepare a setlist for performance. Refine stage presence and technical perfection. Seek feedback from mentors regularly. Maintain consistency throughout.

This plan prioritizes skill growth over arbitrary letter constraints.<|im_end|>
